binaries.title: nwifi.sys (Native WiFi NDIS Miniport Driver) - WIL feature-usage reporting cache dedup guard

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache
key_changes: Added a two-instruction guard `cmp dword ptr [r11+10h], 0 / jnz loc_1C00026F4` at patched address 0x1C0002567, before the three output-record writes (a1[+0x04]=1, a1[+0x08]=a3, a1[+0x0C]=a4).

In the unpatched code, for reporting-kind selector a3 in the band [0x140, 0x17F], after a CAS loop folds the value into the WIL feature-usage cache word *(a2+4) and records in a1[+0x10] whether the value was already present, the function unconditionally restamps the forwarded usage record. The patched build skips that restamp when a1[+0x10] != 0 (value already cached).

This is a WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-usage reporting deduplication refinement. The state operand a2 is &Feature_H2E_WPA3SAE__private_reporting; the caller wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToServiceDirect forwards the record to g_wil_details_recordFeatureUsage and RtlNotifyFeatureUsage (telemetry sinks). No memory-safety operation, no attacker-controlled index/length, no out-of-bounds access.
changed_functions.security_notes: The changed function is wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache, a WIL feature-usage telemetry routine, not a PHY/802.11/NDIS-OID handler. Its a2 argument is the feature-staging variable Feature_H2E_WPA3SAE__private_reporting; the direct caller (wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToServiceDirect @ 0x1C0002708) forwards the produced usage record to g_wil_details_recordFeatureUsage and RtlNotifyFeatureUsage. The patch adds a `cmp dword ptr [r11+10h], 0 / jnz` guard so that when a usage value is already present in the feature-usage cache, the forwarded record is not restamped with a fresh value/index and instead remains zero-initialized (signaling 'already recorded'). The three writes target fixed offsets of a caller-zero-initialized 0x18-byte stack buffer in both builds; there is no attacker-controlled length or index, no out-of-bounds access, no uninitialized read, and no bounds check added or removed. This is feature-usage telemetry bookkeeping / deduplication, consistent with WIL feature-staging servicing churn, and is not a security fix. No CWE applies.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- Unpatched at 0x1C0002567 (unconditional restamp)
+++ Patched at 0x1C0002567 (added dedup guard)

UNPATCHED:
00000001C0002567  mov     [r11+8], r10d      ; a1[+0x08] = a3
00000001C000256B  mov     [r11+4], ebx       ; a1[+0x04] = 1
00000001C000256F  mov     [r11+0Ch], esi     ; a1[+0x0C] = a4
00000001C0002573  jmp     loc_1C00026E9      ; return

PATCHED:
00000001C0002567  cmp     dword ptr [r11+10h], 0   ; already cached?
00000001C000256C  jnz     loc_1C00026F4            ; if so, skip restamp
00000001C0002572  mov     [r11+8], r10d            ; a1[+0x08] = a3 (only if not cached)
00000001C0002576  mov     [r11+4], ebx            ; a1[+0x04] = 1  (only if not cached)
00000001C000257A  mov     [r11+0Ch], esi          ; a1[+0x0C] = a4 (only if not cached)
00000001C000257E  jmp     loc_1C00026F4            ; return

security_findings.entry_point: Internal WIL feature-usage reporting (feature-staging/telemetry). Reached when nwifi records usage of the Feature_H2E_WPA3SAE staging flag via WIL; the caller forwards the result to g_wil_details_recordFeatureUsage and RtlNotifyFeatureUsage. Not an NDIS OID or 802.11 frame-parsing path.
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (WIL feature-usage reporting cache deduplication guard)
security_findings.description: wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache records a feature-usage event into an in-memory cache word and produces a 0x18-byte output usage record for the caller to forward to WIL telemetry sinks. The output record is zero-initialized at entry in both builds. For reporting-kind selector a3 in [0x140, 0x17F], a CAS loop folds the value into the feature-usage cache word *(a2+4) (a2 = &Feature_H2E_WPA3SAE__private_reporting) and writes a1[+0x10] to indicate whether that value was already present. The unpatched build then unconditionally restamps a1[+0x04]=1, a1[+0x08]=a3, a1[+0x0C]=a4. The patched build guards those writes with `if (a1[+0x10] == 0)` so an already-cached (duplicate) usage event no longer restamps the forwarded record. This is a telemetry deduplication refinement with no memory-safety, information-disclosure, or attacker-reachable state-machine impact.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Code-level divergence only (no security trigger): a3 in [0x140, 0x17F] AND the same value already present in the feature-usage cache word (a1[+0x10] != 0). Both builds operate on a caller-zero-initialized 0x18-byte stack buffer and a WIL feature-usage cache word; neither path is driven by attacker-controlled 802.11 frame data or NDIS OID buffer contents, and neither performs an out-of-bounds access.
security_findings.primitive: None. The writes target fixed offsets of a fixed-size, caller-zero-initialized stack buffer. No attacker-controlled index or length, no OOB, no uninitialized read. The delta is a dedup branch, not a bounds check.
security_findings.assembly:
=== UNPATCHED wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache @ 0x1C000243C (CAS loop + changed site) ===
00000001C000254D  xor     edx, edx
00000001C000254F  mov     [r11+10h], edx            ; a1[+0x10] = already-present flag
00000001C0002560  lock cmpxchg [rdi+4], edx         ; atomic update of *(a2+4)
00000001C0002565  jnz     short loc_1C0002538       ; retry
00000001C0002567  mov     [r11+8], r10d             ; a1[+0x08] = a3 (unconditional)
00000001C000256B  mov     [r11+4], ebx              ; a1[+0x04] = 1 (unconditional)
00000001C000256F  mov     [r11+0Ch], esi            ; a1[+0x0C] = a4 (unconditional)
00000001C0002573  jmp     loc_1C00026E9             ; return

=== PATCHED same site (guard added) ===
00000001C0002560  lock cmpxchg [rdi+4], edx
00000001C0002565  jnz     short loc_1C0002538
00000001C0002567  cmp     dword ptr [r11+10h], 0    ; already cached?
00000001C000256C  jnz     loc_1C00026F4             ; if so, skip restamp
00000001C0002572  mov     [r11+8], r10d
00000001C0002576  mov     [r11+4], ebx
00000001C000257A  mov     [r11+0Ch], esi
00000001C000257E  jmp     loc_1C00026F4

=== UNPATCHED entry/dispatch context ===
00000001C000243C  mov     rax, rsp
00000001C0002454  mov     esi, r9d                  ; esi = a4
00000001C0002457  mov     r10d, r8d                 ; r10d = a3 (reporting-kind selector)
00000001C000245A  mov     rdi, rdx                 ; rdi = a2 (feature-usage state ptr)
00000001C000245D  mov     r11, rcx                 ; r11 = a1 (output record ptr)
00000001C0002460  movups  xmmword ptr [rcx], xmm0  ; zero a1[0..0x0F]
00000001C0002463  mov     [rcx+10h], rax           ; zero a1[0x10..0x17]
00000001C0002467  lea     ebx, [rax+1]             ; ebx = 1
00000001C0002518  add     r8d, 0FFFFFEC0h          ; r8d = a3 - 0x140
00000001C000251F  cmp     r8d, 40h                 ; a3 in [0x140, 0x180)?
00000001C0002523  jnb     short loc_1C0002567      ; out of band -> reach writes (a1[+0x10]==0 there)

summary: The only change between the two builds is a two-instruction guard (`cmp dword ptr [r11+10h], 0 / jnz`) added at 0x1C0002567 inside wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache, a WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-usage telemetry routine. The guard suppresses restamping of the forwarded feature-usage record when the value is already present in the feature-usage cache (the Feature_H2E_WPA3SAE feature-staging variable). The caller forwards the record to WIL telemetry sinks (g_wil_details_recordFeatureUsage, RtlNotifyFeatureUsage). An independent normalized whole-binary diff confirms this is the sole difference (0 added / 0 removed functions). The writes target fixed offsets of a caller-zero-initialized fixed-size stack buffer; there is no attacker-controlled index/length, no out-of-bounds access, no uninitialized read, and no bounds check added or removed. This is feature-usage telemetry deduplication / WIL feature-staging servicing churn, not a security fix. No CWE applies; severity: none.
